content.claim: Church-maintained schools and private schools operate under exemptions from state admissions rules, creating a parallel selective-admission channel; a 2015 Curia ruling exempted the Greek Catholic Church from anti-discrimination provisions in the Nyíregyháza case
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Demonstrates institutional durability of church-school autonomy independent of 6/8-year track decision
Contested framing as 'state-motivated church-assisted segregation' reflects advocacy interpretation, not neutral institutional characterization
Does not quantify proportion of national enrollment affected by church-school route
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
